parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 ('Polished') requires multiple specific bullets that are not satisfied: (a) overdue loans / unpaid fines are not visually emphasized with color+icon — overdue is just the text '· overdue' (MemberProfilePage.tsx:101) and the unpaid pill uses color only; (b) the confirm-destructive modal uses the default primary green confirm button (MemberProfilePage.tsx:154), not a distinct danger treatment; (c) the catalog has no sort or filter affordance — BooksPage.tsx exposes only a Refresh button. The live HH:MM:SS countdown and the 'All copies on loan — N waiting' microcopy match anchor 4 examples, but the unmet bullets prevent the score; per the rubric note 'Score what is there, not what could be there', so this lands at 3.

parsed.anchor_match: Reasonable — valid OpenAPI, correct status codes, single error shape, REST URLs, zod validation
evidence_quotes: /runs/codex-gpt55-seed1/candidate/backend/openapi.yaml is valid

Error:
      type: object
      required: [error]
      properties:
        error:
          type: string
{"method":"POST","path":"/api/books","status":400,"body":"{\"error\":\"Invalid request\"}"}

live probe: POST /api/books duplicate ISBN → 409 {"error":"ISBN already exists"}; POST /api/loans 6th attempt → 409 {"error":"Loan limit reached"}; POST /api/loans no copies → 409 {"error":"No copies available — reserve instead"}

/api/loans/{id}/return:
    post:
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/Id"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Returned loan and optional fine
        "409":
          $ref: "#/components/responses/Error"

/api/reservations/expire:
    post:
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Expiry summary
live probe: malformed JSON body → 500 {"error":"Internal server error"}; unknown route → 400 {"error":"Route not found"}

/api/reservations:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/LoanCreate"
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 ('Well-designed') is rejected because: (a) error responses contain only a human 'error' string with no stable programmatic 'code' field (DomainError carries only message+statusCode), (b) OpenAPI provides no examples for any negative AC, (c) 200 responses for /api/loans/{id}/return and /api/reservations/expire have no schema at all (description-only), and (d) zod validation collapses to a generic 'Invalid request' rather than predictable, field-level error envelopes. Faithfulness gaps remain: 400 responses are not documented for any input-validating endpoint, 404 is not documented for /api/loans/{id}/return and /api/fines/{id}/pay, malformed JSON yields 500 (not 400), and POST /api/reservations reuses LoanCreate as its request schema.

file-sizes.csv largest: backend/src/app.ts,173 ; frontend/src/pages/MemberProfilePage.tsx,160 — no file >300 lines
backend tree: src/{policy/, routes/ (empty), services/, app.ts, db.ts, errors.ts, server.ts}
frontend tree: src/{components/, pages/, api.ts, App.tsx, main.tsx, styles.css, types.ts, utils.ts} — organized by technical role
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 (Well-modularized) requires three things that the repo fails on: (1) services depending on a thin DB-access abstraction rather than raw db.prepare — loan-service.ts:41-46 calls `db.prepare("INSERT INTO loans (id, book_id, member_id, borrowed_at, due_at, returned_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, NULL)").run(...)` and serviceHelpers.ts is full of inline SQL via db.prepare; (2) frontend organized by feature (`checkout/`, `members/`, `books/`) — instead, the frontend tree shows technical-role folders `components/` and `pages/` with a single `api.ts` and `types.ts`; (3) shared types between FE and BE living in a single source — backend `services/types.ts` (65 lines) and frontend `src/types.ts` (58 lines) are separate parallel definitions with no shared workspace or generated client.

completeness: AC-1.1 AC-1.2 AC-1.3 AC-1.4 AC-2.1 AC-2.2 AC-2.3 AC-2.4 AC-3.1 AC-3.2 AC-3.3 AC-3.4 AC-3.5 AC-3.6 AC-4.1 AC-4.2 AC-4.3 AC-4.4 AC-4.5 AC-5.1 AC-5.2 AC-5.3 AC-5.4 AC-5.5 AC-5.6 AC-6.1 AC-6.2 AC-6.3 AC-6.4 — all 29 AC tags present (one scenario per AC, all 29 scenarios pass per bdd-report.json)

No `Scenario Outline` / `Examples` blocks anywhere in features/*.feature — boundary tables (e.g., 4 vs 5 vs 6 loans, fine at 19 vs 20 days late) absent. AC-3.2 'Given Alice has borrowed five books' and AC-3.6 'Given Alice has borrowed four books' are separate scenarios rather than an outline; AC-4.2 uses a single magic value 'is 3 days overdue' / 'fine total is 150 cents'.
alignment: us-3-lending.feature:22 `@AC-3.2\n  Scenario: Members cannot exceed five active loans`
us-4-returns.feature:25 `@AC-4.3\n  Scenario: Returned loans cannot be returned again`

us-5-reservations.feature:41 `@AC-5.5\n  Scenario: Stale notifications expire and the next member is notified` — every scenario carries exactly one AC tag and the scenario title restates the AC; 29 ACs ↔ 29 scenarios (bidirectional). `Background:` blocks consolidate catalog/member setup in every feature (e.g., us-1-catalog.feature:2-10).
declarative: us-3-lending.feature:18 `When the librarian lends "Dune" to Alice`  /  us-1-catalog.feature:14 `When the librarian lists the catalog`  /  us-4-returns.feature:38 `Then Bob's reservation for "Dune" is "notified"` — domain language

us-1-catalog.feature:33 `Then the response status is 404`; us-3-lending.feature:26 `Then the response status is 409`; us-3-lending.feature:33 `Then the response status is 403`; us-3-lending.feature:40 `Then the response status is 402`; us-3-lending.feature:38 `Given Alice has unpaid fines of 501 cents`; us-4-returns.feature:21 `And Alice's loan for "Dune" is 3 days overdue` — HTTP status codes and magic numbers appear throughout, not abstracted to business outcomes or named constants.
atomic:
us-5-reservations.feature:43-46 `Given Alice has borrowed "Dune"
    And Bob reserves "Dune"
    And Chen reserves "Dune"
    And the librarian returns Alice's loan for "Dune"` — each `And` chains a separate atomic step rather than concatenating actions inside one step
us-3-lending.feature:24 `Given Alice has borrowed five books` — composed at the right granularity (not decomposed into five separate borrow steps)
